action.hypothesis: JumpReLU at K=128 may be overprovisioned at expansion_factor=8 for the current optimization regime, so reducing expansion_factor to 4 while holding LR=1.6e-3 and AUXK_ALPHA=0.03125 fixed could improve proxy FVU by enforcing a more efficient latent basis.
action.summary: Keep the best K=128 JumpReLU anchor fixed and continue the single-parameter expansion-factor sweep with EXPANSION_FACTOR=4 after EF=16 was only archive-level.
action.self_review: This follows the current exploitation plan and isolates the last untested expansion-factor setting before moving to a different parameter axis. The main risk is reduced capacity worsening reconstruction, but EF=4 is the cleanest remaining test to determine whether EF=8 is actually near-optimal.
notes_to_memory: EXPANSION_FACTOR=16 did not beat the existing JumpReLU proxy anchor, so higher capacity alone is not an obvious path to lower FVU.
This round completes the planned one-variable expansion-factor sweep by testing EXPANSION_FACTOR=4 on the same K=128 JumpReLU baseline.
If EF=4 also fails to improve proxy quality, local JumpReLU expansion-factor search should be considered exhausted and the next round should move to another remaining hyperparameter axis.
next_hypotheses: If EXPANSION_FACTOR=4 improves proxy FVU, promote to full and then refine around the lower-capacity regime before considering cost-focused reductions at smaller K.
If EXPANSION_FACTOR=4 is worse or neutral, finish the current exploitation slate by testing AUXK_ALPHA=0.0625 or LR=2e-4 as the next single-parameter probe.
If the remaining param sweeps also fail, conclude local JumpReLU tuning is exhausted and return to stabilizing an incubating family or introducing a new architecture direction.
